chapter_title.en: Chapter 66 – The fairness and the justice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/343)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/343)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي كَلَامٍ لَهُ أَلَا إِنَّهُ مَنْ يُنْصِفِ النَّاسَ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ لَمْ يَزِدْهُ اللَّهُ إِلَّا عِزّاً .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ibrahim Bin Muhammad Al Saqafy, from Ali Bin Al Moalla, from Yahya Bin Ahmad, from Abu Muhammad Al Maysami, from Rowmiyyi Bin Zurara, from his father,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said in a speech of his<sup>asws</sup>: ‘Indeed! The one is fair with the people from himself, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would not Increase him in anything except for honour’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Ibrahim ibn Muhammad al-Thaqafi from Ali ibn Mu’alla from Yahya ibn Ahmad Fadl ibn Shadhan abu Muhammad al-Maythami from Rumi ibn Zurara from his father from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Amir al-Mu’minin (Ali ibn abu Talib) (a.s.) has said in one of his speeches, ‘You must note that due to a person’s yielding to justice for people against his own self, Allah increases his respect and honor.’”
